choices.text: Ask who was around
Ask about the time window
Ask about prior contact
Press the contradiction
Pin down the exact location
Frame it as the victim bringing trouble
Frame it as pressure and circumstance
Frame it as someone trying to protect something
Frame it as something that got out of hand

nodes.text: If you are asking whether I was near {place}, plenty of people were near {place}. It is a location, not a private chapel.

choices.text: Ask whether anyone else was there
Ask what made the figure stand out

nodes.text: You want me to inventory the whole street now? I noticed movement. I did not conduct a census.
Nothing dramatic. Just somebody moving like they had somewhere to be and did not want commentary on the way there.
{time_phrase}, give or take the margin of error you people always pretend not to have.

choices.text: Ask what happened just before
Ask what happened just after

nodes.text: Before that? Ordinary city noise, ordinary bad weather, ordinary people minding themselves.
After that, I left. That is usually what sensible people do when they suspect the night is turning stupid.
I knew the victim well enough to recognize them and poorly enough not to build a shrine out of it.

choices.text: Ask whether they ever spoke
Ask whether the witness trusted them

nodes.text: We spoke. Adults do that. It does not make every conversation a prelude.
Trust is an inflated word. We had an understanding of distance. That is all.
You say contradiction like it is a magic key. Usually it just means you have decided the answer before the question is done.

choices.text: Tell them the times do not match
Tell them they are withholding the useful part

nodes.text: Then one of your sources is wrong. It happens. You people treat inconsistency like intent when sometimes it is just exhaustion.
What you call useful is usually the part that reads best in court after it has been stripped of context.
Exact location? Near enough to notice, far enough not to owe anybody a play-by-play.

choices.text: Ask for the doorway or side of the building
Ask what the witness could actually see

nodes.text: The side with less foot traffic. You know the kind of place. Every city has ten of them per block.
Enough to understand the atmosphere. Not enough to certify the scene for you.
If you are asking whether the victim knew how to collect trouble, that is a different question from whether they deserved any of it.

choices.text: Ask whether the victim invited the danger
Ask whether the witness is shifting blame

nodes.text: People choose risk every day. Sometimes the bill arrives late and everyone acts surprised by the due date.
I am saying the world was already unstable. If you hear accusation in that, maybe you brought it with you.
Circumstance is the usual accomplice. Thin money, thinner patience, somebody cornered by consequences they postponed too long.

choices.text: Ask what pressure was building
Ask whether the witness is excusing it

nodes.text: Pressure is rarely cinematic. It is usually rent, embarrassment, and one badly timed demand too many.
No. I am describing a mechanism, not offering absolution.
People do ugly things for reasons they insist are noble. Sometimes they even believe themselves.

choices.text: Ask who was supposed to be protected
Ask whether that story sounds convenient

nodes.text: Usually somebody adjacent. Family, reputation, leverage. Pick your favorite idol.
Convenient enough to survive first contact with guilt. Not always convenient enough to survive daylight.
Accidental is a word people reach for when intent would sound expensive. It explains the first second, not always the next ten.

choices.text: Ask whether it looked sudden
Ask whether someone tried to fix it after

nodes.text: Sudden? Possibly. Unthinking? No. People start thinking very hard the second consequences get blood on them.
That would not surprise me. Most panic spends itself cleaning, arranging, or rehearsing.
